methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $23M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Housing & Construction (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Housing & Construction, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Cover
private.rationale: Same sector (Housing & Construction), same stage (Series B)

data.narrative:
Cuby Technologies is a Series B-stage housing and construction company founded by Aleks Gampel and Aleh Kandrashou that has raised $23.4M to date. The Housing & Construction sector comprises a limited comp universe of 2 private peers (ICON and Cover) with no public comparables, constraining the depth of market-based valuation anchors.

The analysis employs stage-progression methodology, which is applicable given the Series B positioning. Using total capital raised of $23.4M as a valuation proxy, a 1.9x step-up multiplier—typical for Series B to next-stage progression—yields an implied valuation of $44M. Peer-median analysis was not viable; the sector contains insufficient same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier comparables to generate a reliable median (only 1 narrow-filter peer identified), making cross-comparable benchmarking unreliable.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $38M (low) to $51M (high), with a median point of $44M. This single-method range carries material uncertainty given the dependency on the stage-progression step-up multiplier and the absence of peer validation. Key risks include execution on go-to-market, the cyclicality inherent to construction, and capital intensity typical of the sector. Future recalibration should incorporate additional comparable transactions or revenue/margin data as the company matures and the comp set expands.
